title: Moe Letter Blues

jokes.text: I moved here 'cause on a calculator, the zip code spells 'boobs.'
jokes.setup: Moe explaining why he moved to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Absurd reason based on calculator letter-number conversion
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist humor with economical delivery that reveals character priorities

jokes.text: It's a town filled with winners and skinners.
jokes.setup: Describing Springfield's residents
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'sinners' referring to Skinner character
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that's functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Handsome, suave, debonair. (sneezes) Hey, get the camera off of me. I'm narrating here.
jokes.setup: Moe describing himself in flattering terms
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with unflattering sneeze and defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with immediate visual contradiction and meta-awareness

jokes.text: Timothy, I finished saying my prayers. Not now, Helen. Today's the day I paint moustaches on my Pullman porters.
jokes.setup: Wife trying to get husband's attention after prayers
jokes.punchline: Husband prioritizing bizarre hobby over wife
jokes.explanation: Absurd hobby detail that characterizes the husband's priorities and neglect

jokes.text: Here's the kind of lullaby my sweet mother sang to me. (woman singing loudly in Hindu, babies crying)
jokes.setup: Character offering to sing a proper lullaby
jokes.punchline: Loud foreign singing that makes babies cry instead of sleep
jokes.explanation: Visual contradiction gag with cultural humor element

jokes.text: I like rajiv, rubaba and big brahmin in the morning! No caste is safe from their merry japes!
jokes.setup: Defending a radio show
jokes.punchline: Pun on morning radio format with Hindu cultural references
jokes.explanation: Culturally specific wordplay that fits Apu's character

jokes.text: Having a ma-hot-ma or man-not-ma contest is not a jape. It is sexist sacrilege!
jokes.setup: Manjula criticizing the radio show content
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Mahatma' while expressing moral outrage
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate moral stance with wordplay, but pun is labored

jokes.text: Are you seein' what I'm seein' here? This omniscient point of view has got some surprising bonuses!
jokes.setup: Moe as narrator becoming aware of his perspective
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to acknowledge narrative device benefits
jokes.explanation: Meta humor that acknowledges the show's narrative structure

jokes.text: I destroyed my body to give birth to you?!
jokes.setup: Mother's Day context
jokes.punchline: Guilt-tripping statement about childbirth sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Standard maternal guilt humor that's recognizable but not original

jokes.text: Tour the historic civil war prison, and picnic where the bodies were once stacked like cordwood.
jokes.setup: Tourism advertisement for family cruise
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely inappropriate historical site for family picnic
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor that contrasts family fun with grim reality

jokes.text: Warning: You may not be amused.
jokes.setup: Advertisement for amusement park
jokes.punchline: Honest warning that the park might not be fun
jokes.explanation: Economical meta-joke about false advertising and low expectations

jokes.text: I like beer, hot dogs, and long blackouts on the beach.
jokes.setup: Homer describing himself to his kids
jokes.punchline: Including alcohol-induced unconsciousness as a hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization mixing innocuous and concerning habits

jokes.text: Sorry, honey. I wasn't listening, and I won't be listening now.
jokes.setup: Marge asking what they were talking about
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest admission of continued inattention
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with perfect economy and brutal honesty

jokes.text: Son, I'm a bit of an expert on the female gender, and I can tell you, women don't mean anything by anything.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about women's meaning
jokes.punchline: Homer's confidently wrong generalization about women
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wisdom that's confidently ignorant

jokes.text: I hear what you're saying, Lisa. (whispers loudly): Nothing.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Homer's attitude toward women
jokes.punchline: Homer pretending to listen while literally saying he hears nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual/verbal gag that demonstrates Homer's selective hearing

jokes.text: And if you get hungry, there's lobster.
jokes.setup: Putting Maggie in playpen for the day
jokes.punchline: Offering expensive seafood to a baby
jokes.explanation: Absurd childcare with economic luxury contrast

jokes.text: One of our wives is leaving us for Moe?! / Moe's leaving town?!
jokes.setup: Reading Moe's letter about running off with someone's wife
jokes.punchline: Different priorities in their reactions to the news
jokes.explanation: Good character differentiation through different concerns

jokes.text: Whatever that Don Pardo gets paid, it ain't enough. / Don Pardo: I make more than you can possibly imagine. And I'm making it right now.
jokes.setup: Moe complaining about voice-over work difficulty
jokes.punchline: Real Don Pardo responding with boastful earnings claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke with real celebrity reference and industry humor

jokes.text: Which one of our wives got Moe-jacked?
jokes.setup: Wondering which wife left with Moe
jokes.punchline: Pun combining Moe's name with 'hijacked'
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun that works in context but isn't exceptional

jokes.text: You're losing me, or you're leaving me?! / We're breaking up!
jokes.setup: Poor phone connection during emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous meaning of 'breaking up' - connection vs relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning that creates perfect dramatic irony

jokes.text: Most of our cargo is explosives.
jokes.setup: Ferry accident with people screaming
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of explosive cargo after crash
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through casual delivery of dangerous information

jokes.text: When there's cruelty involved, Bart sure knows his history.
jokes.setup: Watching Bart engage with Civil War reenactment
jokes.punchline: Homer's insight that Bart only learns when violence is involved
jokes.explanation: Great character observation about Bart's interests - both accurate and funny.

jokes.text: We just started a new jigsaw puzzle last week. We're still on the edges.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Helen wouldn't leave him
jokes.punchline: Treating puzzle progress as relationship commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing his boring relationship priorities

jokes.text: When a woman is peaceful and content, that means all hell is about to break loose.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he and Marge don't fight
jokes.punchline: Apu's cynical marriage wisdom
jokes.explanation: Relatable marriage observation that fits Apu's experience

jokes.text: Think back, think back, think back... (rattling) Not that far back.
jokes.setup: Apu telling Homer to remember when problems started
jokes.punchline: Homer going too far back in memory
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Homer's literal interpretation

jokes.text: Hey, chunky with the pants of meatballs. Take our picture.
jokes.setup: Asking Homer to take their photo
jokes.punchline: Creatively insulting description of Homer
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's characteristically mean from Patty/Selma

jokes.text: He can't do anything right. Except setting off car alarms with his big fat footsteps.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Homer's incompetence
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his weight causing car alarms
jokes.explanation: Typical Patty/Selma cruelty with visual payoff of car alarms actually going off.

jokes.text: Go ugly up someone else's house, you penis-curling she-devils!
jokes.setup: Homer getting frustrated with photo session
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and crude insult
jokes.explanation: Memorably crude Homer outburst with creative anatomical reference

jokes.text: You had that scar before you got on. / What scar? Oh, you'll find out.
jokes.setup: Ferry operator denying responsibility for injury
jokes.punchline: Passenger doesn't know about impending injury
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with good misdirection about inevitable danger

jokes.text: All right, calm down there, 'Pu and she-Pu.
jokes.setup: Apu and Manjula arguing at the bar
jokes.punchline: Moe's nickname adaptation for the couple
jokes.explanation: Typical Moe wordplay that shows his casual racism/cultural insensitivity

jokes.text: You see this? I'm playing the world's smallest sitar for you.
jokes.setup: Responding to Manjula's complaints about her day
jokes.punchline: Cultural adaptation of 'world's smallest violin' gesture
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural adaptation of classic sarcastic gesture

jokes.text: If only that mark on your forehead was an off button!
jokes.setup: Manjula frustrated with Apu
jokes.punchline: Reference to Apu's bindi as technological control
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference combined with marital frustration

jokes.text: My eyes have more bags than the Darjeeling limited.
jokes.setup: Manjula describing how tired she looks
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'bags under eyes' and luggage on train
jokes.explanation: Clever pun with cultural reference that fits character's education

jokes.text: My nards! My nards! My nards!
jokes.setup: Video entertainment for men at the bar
jokes.punchline: Repetitive exclamation of groin injury
jokes.explanation: Lowbrow physical comedy that's predictable and relies on cheap laughs

jokes.text: Today's the first, which means it was last month, Which means you're being ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she's still mad from last night
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical calendar-based dismissal of her anger
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - using arbitrary time boundaries to avoid responsibility.

jokes.text: Way to bitch up the conversation, Helen.
jokes.setup: Helen making observation about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Timothy's inappropriate response to his wife
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate rudeness that shows marriage problems

jokes.text: Where a clam has only one pearl, she has many.
jokes.setup: Comparing Helen to a happy clam
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic extension of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Timothy's attempt at poetry reveals his pretentious side.

jokes.text: Now, don't you jive me, Tim. Just take your thumb and put it over her mouth. (growling) See? Looks like that she-cano is about to blow!
jokes.setup: Moe teaching Timothy about reading his wife's mood
jokes.punchline: Visual trick revealing Helen's angry expression plus volcano pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag with strong pun and relationship insight

jokes.text: * Krakatoa, East of Java * never spewed a hotter lava * than a mount Saint Helen who's mad at you *
jokes.setup: Following up the volcano metaphor
jokes.punchline: Improvised song with geological puns about angry Helen
jokes.explanation: Extended volcano metaphor with decent wordplay but overly long

jokes.text: Oh, nobody told me I had to drive the bus back. I've mistimed some substances!
jokes.setup: Bus needing to return to town urgently
jokes.punchline: Otto's drug-timing problem affecting his driving ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto character moment - casual about drug use affecting his job.

jokes.text: A woman her age married to a man of-- How old are you, 65? / Thirty-nine. / Dear God, this day gets more and more disturbing.
jokes.setup: Apu commenting on age difference in Homer's marriage
jokes.punchline: Homer's actual age being more shocking than assumed
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke that subverts expectations about Homer's appearance vs age

jokes.text: Marge, you're not leaving with Moe is the best thing that never happened to me.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing relief that Marge isn't leaving
jokes.punchline: Confusing double negative construction
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer verbal confusion that fits his character

jokes.text: Even I've hooked up with your old lady. / Sorry, kid. You were my favorite Uncle, Uncle Otto.
jokes.setup: Otto casually mentioning romantic history
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's heartbroken response about lost innocence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about childhood innocence lost through adult revelation

jokes.text: Is that a train in your pocket or are you just happy to see me? / Both.
jokes.setup: Adaptation of classic Mae West line about train tickets
jokes.punchline: Timothy's literal confirmation of both interpretations
jokes.explanation: Classic setup with decent adaptation to train travel context

jokes.text: I feel the pain of a thousand squishee headaches.
jokes.setup: Apu expressing emotional pain
jokes.punchline: Cultural adaptation using his signature product
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific metaphor that ties to his work

jokes.text: What am I, blind and noseless?
jokes.setup: Apu asking if she's leaving him for Moe
jokes.punchline: Manjula's brutal assessment of Moe's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult that efficiently dismisses Moe's attractiveness

jokes.text: The tastiest ice cream is the hardest to scoop. But if you wrap something warm around it...
jokes.setup: Apu philosophizing about marriage difficulty
jokes.punchline: Ice cream metaphor with warmth solution
jokes.explanation: Sweet metaphor that fits Apu's wisdom and retail background

jokes.text: I bought some roadside corn, ate it raw, got a kernel in my teeth, flossed with the corn silk
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his activities
jokes.punchline: Gross improvised dental hygiene with corn
jokes.explanation: Characteristically gross Moe behavior that's absurdly detailed

jokes.text: Think of it as a wake-up call from a man who ain't got nothin' but a blow-up doll. And even she left me. Should not have used helium.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining his motivation for the letter
jokes.punchline: Escalating pathetic details about his loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment with escalating pathetic details and great final twist

jokes.text: I don't know whether to punch you or kiss you. So I'm gonna do both.
jokes.setup: Homer's conflicted feelings about Moe's help
jokes.punchline: Literal execution of both impulses
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer literal solution to emotional conflict

tmdb_overview: As Mother's Day approaches, Moe narrates an episode in which he writes a letter to Homer, Apu and Reverend Lovejoy, who are vacationing with their children, and threatens to run away with one of their wives. While the trio tries to determine whose wife Moe is referring to, Homer, Lovejoy and Apu flashback to the intimate moments they initially ignored between Moe and Marge, Manjula and Helen Lovejoy. But when the boys return from their trip, they're in for the surprise of their lives.
